summary: Methylphenidate may indirectly contribute to skin picking by worsening preexisting obsessions or compulsions through its effects on CNS activity; however, no direct causal relationship has been established between methylphenidate use and this behavior. This risk should prompt clinicians to monitor patients for emerging psychomotor disturbances during therapy.
